steps.title: Calculate first-year earnings
steps.content: Account A: $$0.06 \times 500 = 30$$ per year. Account B: fixed \$25 per year. In year 1, Account A earns more.

steps.title: Observe the growth pattern
steps.content: As Account A's balance grows through compounding, its annual earnings grow too. Year 2: balance is \$530, so earnings are $$0.06 \times 530 = 31.80$$. Year 3: balance is \$561.80, so earnings are $$0.06 \times 561.80 \approx 33.71$$. Account B's earnings remain \$25 every year.

steps.title: Conclusion
steps.content: Account A starts earning \$30/year and grows exponentially each year (to \$31.80, \$33.71, …). Account B earns a constant \$25/year. Since Account A earns more initially and its earnings grow while B's are fixed, Account A always earns more per year. That matches choice A — A is correct.
